text.title: practices-python-ecosystem Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for practices-python-ecosystem. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: FastAPI route handlers MUST use Pydantic BaseModel for request/response bodies, Depends() for injecting DB sessions and auth, and delegate business logic to a service function. Route handlers MUST NOT contain inline DB queries or business logic.
rule.rationale: FastAPI's Depends() system makes auth and DB injection testable via override. Pydantic models provide automatic validation and OpenAPI schema generation. Agents often place logic inline in route handlers when not explicitly constrained.

rule.statement: Python projects MUST use the `src/` layout: source code under `src/<package>/`, tests under `tests/` at the project root, `pyproject.toml` for metadata, and `.env.example` committed (never `.env`). `conftest.py` lives in `tests/`.
rule.rationale: The `src/` layout prevents the package from being importable from the project root without installation, catching missing `__init__.py` and install issues early. It is the layout recommended by pip, uv, and poetry for 2024+.

rule.statement: Every public Python function MUST have complete type annotations: parameter types and return type. Use `from typing import Optional, List, Dict` (Python 3.8 compatible) or native `X | None`, `list[X]`, `dict[K, V]` syntax (Python 3.10+). Bare `def f(x)` with no annotations fails ruff's type-check rules.
rule.rationale: Type hints enable pyright/mypy static analysis, improve agent completions, and serve as inline documentation that cannot drift from the implementation. Missing hints force agents to guess return shapes and produce incorrect downstream code.

rule.statement: Python projects MUST use `pyproject.toml` for project metadata and configuration. Dependency management MUST use uv (preferred 2026) or poetry — never raw `pip install` without a lockfile. The lockfile (`uv.lock` or `poetry.lock`) MUST be committed. Dev dependencies MUST be separated from runtime dependencies.
rule.rationale: Lockfiles guarantee reproducible installs across developer machines and CI. Without a lockfile, agents install the latest versions which may be incompatible, producing "works on my machine" failures. uv is 10-100x faster than pip and is the standard for 2026.
